channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: San Jose, SJSU announce collaboration with NVIDIA to further workforce development, AI innovation
item.description: Nvidia signed two memorandums of understanding on Thursday -- including he first workforce pipeline initiative in the company’s history with a city government -- that offer training programs to both entities to upskill the workforce and drive innovation in San Jose.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: SolarWinds Cyber Attack Aftermath: Agencies Address Gaps, Prepare for Future Threats
item.description: Four years after the SolarWinds cyber attack, federal agencies have closed many of the gaps that made the incident possible. CISA's Jeff Greene discusses the impact and progress since the attack.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Refurbished Steam Deck OLEDs are now almost as cheap as new LCD models – good luck finding one though
item.description: Ignoring the Steam Deck OLED isn't much of an option now, because the refurbished models are almost as cheap as new LCD models.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: iOS 18.2 Brings Enterprise IT Admins Control Over Apple Intelligence Features
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update introduces significant improvements for enterprise IT admins, including control over Apple Intelligence features and more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash is requiring its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok's future in jeopardy as US ban looms
item.description: TikTok’s future in the U.S. is in serious jeopardy after a federal appeals court rejected its push to overturn a law that could ban the app next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AWS adds Graviton Savings Dashboard to help enterprises optimize infra costs
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched a dashboard for its Arm-based Graviton CPU that will measure savings opportunities in an effort to help enterprises further optimize their expenditure on subscribed infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok’s Canada unit wants court review of shut-down order
item.description: TikTok’s Canadian unit said it has filed an emergency motion with Canada’s Federal Court seeking a judicial review of an order that the company shut down its operations in the country on national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Deloitte’s annual tech trends report: AI will soon be in everything
item.description: Artificial intelligence is heading toward a future where it’s so embedded in everything we do, we’ll eventually forget it’s even there.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Google unveils Gemini 2.0 AI model for agentic era
item.description: Emphasizing a new AI model for the agentic era, Google has introduced Gemini 2.0, which the company calls its most capable model yet. Announced December 11, the Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental model will be available to all Gemini users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to protect your communications through encryption
item.description: Cyber officials are advising people to use encryption in their communications after a sprawling hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ethical hacker explains the risks of using public internet
item.description: In the modern world we are all constantly connected, but this comes with risks. As most cybersecurity specialists will tell you, the biggest vulnerability in any system is the user—whether at home or work.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: GM shuts down robotaxi project to focus on autonomous vehicles
item.description: General Motors (GM) said Tuesday that it will no longer fund the development of robotaxis and instead focus on creating fully autonomous personal vehicles. Cruise, GM’s self-driving car subsidiary, will be combined with the automaker’s own team to form a single unit focused on autonomous driving.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: 8 brilliant browser tab tricks for Windows power users
item.description: Web browsers are essential for everything from business productivity to social media and online shopping. Whether you use Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Brave, or something even more exotic, there’s a good chance your web browser is your most used Windows program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Android 2025 preview: 7 things to watch
item.description: As we look ahead to a whole new year of green-tinted Googley goodness, I’m feeling both guardedly excited — and curiously like Bill Murray in the movie Groundhog Day. Google’s got some pretty weighty pivots in the works for the Android arena right now.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IC’s new OSINT standards cover open source data, AI services
item.description: As OSINT becomes a major focus for the intelligence community, the new standards give analysts and collectors explicit instructions for citing certain data. The post IC’s new OSINT standards cover open source data, AI services first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows iPhone users how to easily delete multiple emails and manage their inboxes to reduce digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Supply chain compromise of Ultralytics AI library results in trojanized versions
item.description: Attackers have compromised Ultralytics YOLO packages published on PyPI, the official Python package index, by compromising the build environment of the popular library for creating custom machine learning models.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
